description: HARD-CASE document set for Stage 1a: continental drift circa 1915, modeling the historical moment BEFORE the drift hypothesis was accepted. Reliability reflects each source's STANDING AT THE TIME (dismissed monograph = low, establishment consensus = high, field surveys = moderate), NOT modern hindsight and NOT reverse-engineered to make the engine win. The correct claim is deliberately the low-reliability underdog; ground_truth marks it because it is actually correct. Separate topic label and file so the existing corpus is untouched.

documents.source: Drifting-continents monograph (1915), dismissed by the geological establishment of the day
Authoritative geology textbook, early-20th-century fixist consensus
Cross-Atlantic rock-strata and glacial-deposit field notes
